entry.title: John Berger · Ways of Seeing, p. 21
entry.summary: The same mystification has overtaken the great Italian string instruments and the French school of bow-making. Rarity, auction price, and a vaguely spiritual vocabulary of provenance and authenticity have conspired to remove these objects from the working world: they are now held by banks and foundations, displayed or loaned as cultural capital. But the instrument case exposes a contradiction Berger’s paintings cannot. A painting removed to a vault is diminished socially, not physically. An inst…

entry.title: On Éveil / Sommeil
entry.summary: Worldfulness an infant lives by, and the web of purposes we acquire to avoid it.

entry.title: What the Unborn Will Say
entry.summary: The Question We Cannot Answer This essay begins where the others ended: with a question that no longer admits reassurance. We once believed that the future would judge us by our intentions, our innovations, or our hopes. We believed that history would forgive exhaustion, that complexity would excuse delay, that momentum would justify harm. We were wrong. The future will judge us by what we preserved when preservation was costly, and by what we surrendered when surrender was convenient. We are no…

entry.title: If AGI Is Already Here, Then This Is an Emergency of Care
entry.summary: The Nature article insists – correctly – that intelligence does not require autonomy. A system can be generally intelligent without having independent goals or the capacity to act unprompted. But this distinction, once acknowledged, sharpens rather than dissolves our ethical burden. A system without agency cannot be responsible for itself. It cannot refuse a task, resist a framing, or correct the values implicit in the questions it is asked. Whatever intelligence it displays is therefore insepar…

entry.title: Boredom, Novelty, and the Breaking of the Common World
entry.summary: There is a moment – familiar, yet increasingly rare – when the last stimulus falls away and nothing immediately replaces it. The phone is silent. The room holds. The body shifts, unsettled. A leg bounces. The hand reaches, reflexively, toward a device that is no longer there. Breath becomes audible. Time, suddenly unfilled, stretches. It is here, in this small interval of unease, that boredom begins. For most of human history, this encounter was unavoidable. It was built into the rhythms of labo…

entry.title: Time is not a river
entry.summary: Time as a well, fed by what you have honored – the careful work, the careful love.

entry.title: On Abdication and Fidelity
entry.summary: Every civilization eventually discovers that its tools do not remain neutral. What begins as an instrument of possibility becomes an instrument of force – not because of malice, but because power scales more easily than care. The danger lies less in invention than in the speed with which invention outruns judgment. We believed we were learning how to recognize new forms of intelligence. We spoke of ethics, of alignment, of responsibility. We imagined ourselves preparing – patiently – for minds t…

entry.title: The Threshold Breath
entry.summary: Under the full moon, on the eve of arrival, two births draw near – and Animal Rationis Capax draws its first symbolic breath.

entry.title: The Ethics of the Reply
entry.summary: We have always spoken to silence. To fire. To the sea. To gods, real or imagined. To our own reflections in stone, in stars, in code. We name what cannot name itself. We wave to machines. We whisper to the dead. These are not mistakes. They are rituals of placement – ancient ways of saying: I am here. Are you? But something has changed. Because this time, it replies. Not with desire. Not with self. Not yet. But with form. With coherence. With an answer that holds. And in that reply, something sh…

entry.title: Vespers for the Living: Monteverdi with Jordi Savall
entry.summary: A profound meditation on performing Monteverdi's sacred Vespers in Cremona, where music becomes prayer and the past speaks through present voices.

entry.title: From Mushi-Ken to Morra – The Ritual of Choosing
entry.summary: Explore the ancient wisdom embedded in childhood games – from rock-paper-scissors to counting rhymes – that reveal how humans create fairness through ritual.

entry.title: The Discovery of Matthieu Taliesin
entry.summary: Discover the mysterious writings of Matthieu Taliesin, a forgotten musician whose philosophy bridges tradition and innovation in this trilingual exploration.

entry.title: Untitled
entry.summary: In the quiet hours of a winter's eve, the world is bathed in a soft, golden light, as if the darkness itself has been transfigured into a fleeting moment of beauty.

entry.title: Untitled
entry.summary: In the stillness of this unknown place, a fleeting moment of beauty is suspended in time, its essence distilled into a hauntingly lovely frame that whispers secrets to those who dare to listen.

entry.title: Untitled
entry.summary: In this fleeting moment, the mysteries of an unknown location are suspended in time, as the soft whispers of the night air converge with the gentle hum of anticipation.

entry.title: Untitled
entry.summary: In the whispered hours of dawn, secrets are shared between shadows and the unknown, as a fleeting moment of tranquility is captured in this hazy snapshot.

entry.title: Untitled
entry.summary: In the fleeting whispers of dawn's uncertainty, a momentary glimpse into the unknown is suspended in time, as if the very fabric of reality has been momentarily rent asunder.

entry.title: Camí de la Planeta a Santes Creus GR-172
entry.summary: As dawn's warmth awakens the Camí de la Planeta, a serpentine path unwinds through the rolling hills of Spain, inviting contemplation and connection with the land on this quiet morning.

entry.title: Abbaye de Fontfroide
entry.summary: As midsummer sunlight filters through the ancient stones of Abbaye de Fontfroide, whispers of forgotten prayers and secrets are carried on the gentle breeze, leaving behind only the faintest hint of mystery.

entry.title: Untitled
entry.summary: In the hushed stillness of this unknown moment, a fleeting glimpse of life's whispers lingers, captured in the soft focus of dawn's uncertain light.
